revision.text:
{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐資時 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]中期 | 生誕 = [[弘長]]3年（[[1263年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年[[6月29日 (旧暦)|6月29日]]（[[1281年]][[7月16日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町 | 官位 = 備中権守、贈[[従四位]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] [[壱岐国]][[守護代]] | 主君 = [[惟康親王]] | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[少弐経資]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[少弐氏|少弐]]一族の武将。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 人物 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[文永の役]]で、叔父の[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣した。弘安4年（1281年）の[[弘安の役]]では、壱岐国瀬戸（現・壱岐市芦辺町）の船匿城に、父の少弐経資の代理として居たが、蒙古軍による船匿城攻めの戦闘にて討ち死にする。享年19歳。兄弟の[[小弐景資]]は、[[鷹島]]で[[弘安の役]]を戦った。
== その他 == [[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。[[1997年]]、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して芦辺港フェリーターミナルに建立された。
== 関連項目 == *[[壱岐神社]] *[[元寇]] *[[壱岐]] *[[鷹島]] *[[小弐景資]]
== 出典・参考文献== *「壱岐の風土と歴史」[[中上史行]]（自費出版）1995年初版、2002年第2版<!-- 壱岐市文化会館蔵 --> *[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunka/hyakusen/iki_tusima/025.html 長崎文化ジャクション] *[http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html 少弐資時の墓の旅行] *[http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ 壱岐神社・壱岐護国神社]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しように すけとき}} [[Category:鎌倉時代の武士]] [[Category:少弐氏|すけとき]] [[Category:壱岐国の人物]] [[Category:日本の神 (人物神)]] [[Category:1263年生]] [[Category:1281年没]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[少弐氏|少弐]]一族の武将。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 人物 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[文永の役]]で、叔父の[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣した。弘安4年（1281年）の[[弘安の役]]では、祖父の[[少弐資能]]や父の[[少弐経資]]らとともに日本軍を率いて壱岐島を占領する東路軍に対する攻撃で戦死する。享年19。祖父の[[少弐資能]]や父の[[少弐経資]]もこの戦闘で負傷した。この壱岐島の戦いで敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父の[[少弐景資]]は、[[鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。
== その他 == [[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。[[1997年]]、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して芦辺港フェリーターミナルに建立された。
== 関連項目 == *[[壱岐神社]] *[[元寇]] *[[壱岐]] *[[鷹島]] *[[少弐景資]]
== 出典・参考文献== *「壱岐の風土と歴史」[[中上史行]]（自費出版）1995年初版、2002年第2版<!-- 壱岐市文化会館蔵 --> *[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunka/hyakusen/iki_tusima/025.html 長崎文化ジャクション] *[http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html 少弐資時の墓の旅行] *[http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ 壱岐神社・壱岐護国神社]

'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[少弐氏|少弐]]一族の武将。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 人物 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[文永の役]]で、叔父の[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣した。弘安4年（1281年）の[[弘安の役]]では、祖父の[[少弐資能]]や父の[[少弐経資]]らとともに日本軍を率いて壱岐島を占領する東路軍に対する攻撃で戦死する。享年19。祖父の[[少弐資能]]や父の[[少弐経資]]もこの戦闘で負傷した。この壱岐島の戦いで敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父の[[少弐景資]]は、[[鷹島_(長崎県)|鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。
== その他 == [[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。[[1997年]]、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して芦辺港フェリーターミナルに建立された。
== 関連項目 == *[[壱岐神社]] *[[鷹島_(長崎県)]] *[[少弐景資]] *[[新城神社]]
== 出典・参考文献== *「壱岐の風土と歴史」[[中上史行]]（自費出版）1995年初版、2002年第2版<!-- 壱岐市文化会館蔵 --> *[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunka/hyakusen/iki_tusima/025.html 長崎文化ジャクション] *[http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html 少弐資時の墓の旅行] *[http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ 壱岐神社・壱岐護国神社]

== 生涯 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]で、叔父・[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣を果たした。弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]では、祖父・[[少弐資能|資能]]や父・経資らと共に日本軍を率いて、壱岐島を占領する東路軍と戦うが壱岐島前の海上で戦死する。享年19。

'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[武将]]。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 生涯 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]で、叔父・[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣を果たした。弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]では、祖父・[[少弐資能|資能]]や父・経資らと共に日本軍を率いて、壱岐島を占領する東路軍と戦うが壱岐島前の海上で戦死する。享年19。
この[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]で敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父・景資は、[[鷹島 (長崎県)|鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）[[11月10日]]、従四位の位階が追贈された。
[[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。[[1997年]]、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して芦辺港フェリーターミナルに建立された。
==関連史跡== *[[少弐資時]]の墓（壱岐市芦辺町） :元寇の二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐資時 | 画像 = Suketoki Shoni Statue.jpg | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = 壱岐市にある少弐資時像 | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]中期 | 生誕 = [[弘長]]3年（[[1263年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年[[6月29日 (旧暦)|6月29日]]（[[1281年]][[7月16日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町 | 官位 = [[備中国|備中]][[国司|権守]]、贈[[従四位]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] [[壱岐国|壱岐]][[守護代]] | 主君 = [[惟康親王]] | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[少弐経資]] | 兄弟 = '''資時'''、[[少弐盛経|盛経]]<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇 391頁</ref>、[[少弐時経|時経]]、[[少弐盛氏|盛氏]]、[[少弐貞資|貞資]] | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[武将]]。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 生涯 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]で、叔父・[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣を果たした。弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]では、祖父・[[少弐資能|資能]]や父・経資らと共に日本軍を率いて、壱岐島を占領する東路軍と戦うが壱岐島前の海上で戦死する。享年19。
この[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]で敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父・景資は、[[鷹島 (長崎県)|鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）[[11月10日]]、従四位の位階が追贈された。
[[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。[[1997年]]、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して[[芦辺漁港|芦辺港]]フェリーターミナル前に建立された。
==関連史跡== *'''少弐資時の墓'''（壱岐市芦辺町） :壱岐神社そばに少弐資時の墓がある。周辺は「少弐公園」として整備されており、弘安の役古戦場を示す碑や、当時の日本船のものと思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる[[防人]]の[[狼煙]]台がある。
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|少弐資時の墓 File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|碇石 File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|狼煙台 </gallery>

'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[武将]]。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 生涯 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[元寇#文永の役|文永の役]]で、叔父・[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣を果たした。弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇#弘安の役|弘安の役]]では、祖父・[[少弐資能|資能]]や父・経資らと共に日本軍を率いて、壱岐島を占領する東路軍との奮闘の末、戦死する。享年19。
この[[元寇#壱岐島の戦い|壱岐島の戦い]]で敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父・景資は、[[鷹島 (長崎県)|鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）[[11月10日]]、従四位の位階が追贈された<ref>田尻佐 編『贈位諸賢伝 増補版 上』（近藤出版社、1975年）特旨贈位年表 p.34</ref>。
[[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。
==関連史跡== *'''少弐資時の墓'''（壱岐市芦辺町瀬戸浦） :壱岐神社そばに少弐資時の墓がある。周辺は「少弐公園」として整備されており、弘安の役古戦場を示す碑や、当時の日本船のものと思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる[[防人]]の[[狼煙]]台がある。
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|少弐資時の墓 File:Shoni_Park.jpg|小弐公園入口 File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|弘安の役古戦場 File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|碇石 File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|狼煙台 </gallery> *'''船匿城'''(ふなかくしじょう) {{節stub}} ==その他== *[[1997年]]（平成9年）、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して[[芦辺漁港|芦辺港]]フェリーターミナル前に建立された。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} <references/>
== 出典 == *「壱岐の風土と歴史」[[中上史行]]（自費出版）1995年初版、2002年第2版<!-- 壱岐市文化会館蔵 --> *『[[尊卑分脈]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[鷹島_(長崎県)]] *[[少弐景資]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html 少弐資時の墓の旅行] *[http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ 壱岐神社・壱岐護国神社]

== 生涯 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[元寇|文永の役]]で、叔父・[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で初陣を果たした。弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇|弘安の役]]では、祖父・[[少弐資能|資能]]や父・経資らと共に日本軍を率いて、壱岐島を占領する東路軍との奮闘の末、戦死する。享年19。
この[[元寇|壱岐島の戦い]]で敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父・景資は、[[鷹島 (長崎県)|鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）[[11月10日]]、従四位の位階が追贈された<ref>田尻佐 編『贈位諸賢伝 増補版 上』（近藤出版社、1975年）特旨贈位年表 p.34</ref>。

revision.text:
{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Suketoki | image = Suketoki Shoni Statue.jpg | image_size = 220px | image_caption = Statue of Shōni Suketoki in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}} | era = Mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kōchō 3|ja|弘長3rd year }} (1263) | death_date = {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year }} [[June 29 (lunar calendar)|June 29]] (July 16, 1281) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | grave = {{ill|Ashibe Town|ja|芦辺町}}, Iki City, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | rank = {{ill|Governor|ja|権守}} of {{ill|Bitchū Province|ja|備中国}}, posthumously awarded {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}, Deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国}} | master = {{ill|Prince Koreyasu|ja|惟康親王}} | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資}} | siblings = Suketoki, {{ill|Shōni Moritsune|ja|少弐盛経}}, {{ill|Shōni Tokitsune|ja|少弐時経}}, {{ill|Shōni Morishige|ja|少弐盛氏}}, {{ill|Shōni Sadatsune|ja|少弐貞資}} | spouse = | children = | notable_battles = {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} ({{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year }} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|文永の役}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year }} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|ja|平戸島}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島}}. In {{ill|Taishō 4|ja|大正4th year }} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|smoke signal|ja|狼煙}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|ja|平成9th year }}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * "The Climate and History of Iki," {{ill|Nakagami Fumiyuki|ja|中上史行}} (self-published, 1995, 2002) * {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島_(長崎県)}} * {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資}}
== External Links == * [http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html Visit the Grave of Shōni Suketoki] * [http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ Iki Shrine and Iki Gokoku Shrine]

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Suketoki | image = Suketoki Shoni Statue.jpg | image_size = 220px | image_caption = Statue of Shōni Suketoki in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}} | era = Mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kōchō 3|ja|弘長3rd year|12=draft}} (1263) | death_date = {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year|12=draft}} [[June 29 (lunar calendar)|June 29]] (July 16, 1281) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | grave = {{ill|Ashibe Town|ja|芦辺町|12=draft}}, Iki City, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} | rank = {{ill|Governor|ja|権守|12=draft}} of {{ill|Bitchū Province|ja|備中国|12=draft}}, posthumously awarded {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft}}, Deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国|12=draft}} | master = {{ill|Prince Koreyasu|ja|惟康親王|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}} | siblings = Suketoki, {{ill|Shōni Moritsune|ja|少弐盛経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Tokitsune|ja|少弐時経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Morishige|ja|少弐盛氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Sadatsune|ja|少弐貞資|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = | notable_battles = {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}} ({{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=draft}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=draft}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国|12=draft}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year|12=draft}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|文永の役|12=draft}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year|12=draft}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=draft}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=draft}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|ja|平戸島|12=draft}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Taishō 4|ja|大正4th year|12=draft}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=draft}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=draft}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|smoke signal|ja|狼煙|12=draft}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|ja|平成9th year|12=draft}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=draft}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * "The Climate and History of Iki," {{ill|Nakagami Fumiyuki|ja|中上史行|12=draft}} (self-published, 1995, 2002) * {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|12=draft}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島_(長崎県)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html Visit the Grave of Shōni Suketoki] * [http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ Iki Shrine and Iki Gokoku Shrine]

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Suketoki | image = Suketoki Shoni Statue.jpg | image_size = 220px | image_caption = Statue of Shōni Suketoki in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}} | era = Mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kōchō 3|ja|弘長3rd year|12=draft}} (1263) | death_date = {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year|12=draft}} [[June 29 (lunar calendar)|June 29]] (July 16, 1281) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | grave = {{ill|Ashibe Town|ja|芦辺町|12=draft}}, Iki City, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} | rank = {{ill|Governor|ja|権守|12=draft}} of {{ill|Bitchū Province|ja|備中国|12=draft}}, posthumously awarded {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft}}, Deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国|12=draft}} | master = {{ill|Prince Koreyasu|ja|惟康親王|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}} | siblings = Suketoki, {{ill|Shōni Moritsune|ja|少弐盛経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Tokitsune|ja|少弐時経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Morishige|ja|少弐盛氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Sadatsune|ja|少弐貞資|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = | notable_battles = {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}} ({{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=draft}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=draft}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国|12=draft}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year|12=draft}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|文永の役|12=draft}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year|12=draft}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=draft}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=draft}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|ja|平戸島|12=draft}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Taishō 4|ja|大正4th year|12=draft}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=draft}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=draft}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|smoke signal|ja|狼煙|12=draft}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|ja|平成9th year|12=draft}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=draft}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * "The Climate and History of Iki," {{ill|Nakagami Fumiyuki|ja|中上史行|12=draft}} (self-published, 1995, 2002) * {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|12=draft}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島_(長崎県)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}

{{Infobox samurai | name = Shōni Suketoki | image = Suketoki Shoni Statue.jpg | image_size = 220px | image_caption = Statue of Shōni Suketoki in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}} | era = Mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kōchō 3|ja|弘長3rd year|12=draft}} (1263) | death_date = {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year|12=draft}} [[June 29 (lunar calendar)|June 29]] (July 16, 1281) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | grave = {{ill|Ashibe Town|ja|芦辺町|12=draft}}, Iki City, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} | rank = {{ill|Governor|ja|権守|12=draft}} of {{ill|Bitchū Province|ja|備中国|12=draft}}, posthumously awarded {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft}}, Deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国|12=draft}} | master = {{ill|Prince Koreyasu|ja|惟康親王|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|ja|少弐氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}} | siblings = Suketoki, {{ill|Shōni Moritsune|ja|少弐盛経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Tokitsune|ja|少弐時経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Morishige|ja|少弐盛氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōni Sadatsune|ja|少弐貞資|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = | notable_battles = {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}} ({{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=draft}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=draft}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=draft}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|ja|守護|12=draft}} of {{ill|Iki Province|ja|壱岐国|12=draft}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year|12=draft}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|文永の役|12=draft}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4th year|12=draft}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=draft}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=draft}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|ja|平戸島|12=draft}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Taishō 4|ja|大正4th year|12=draft}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=draft}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=draft}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|ja|弘安の役|12=draft}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|smoke signal|ja|狼煙|12=draft}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|ja|平成9th year|12=draft}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=draft}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}.

<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|ja|平成9th year|12=draft}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=draft}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * "The Climate and History of Iki," {{ill|Nakagami Fumiyuki|ja|中上史行|12=draft}} (self-published, 1995, 2002) * {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|12=draft}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島_(長崎県)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html Visit the Grave of Shōni Suketoki] * [http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ Iki Shrine and Iki Gokoku Shrine] <!--Shōni Suketoki-->

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 少弐 資時 | 画像 = Suketoki Shoni Statue.jpg | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = [[壱岐市]]にある少弐資時像 | 時代 = [[鎌倉時代]]中期 | 生誕 = [[弘長]]3年（[[1263年]]） | 死没 = [[弘安]]4年[[6月29日 (旧暦)|6月29日]]（[[1281年]][[7月16日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町 | 官位 = [[備中国|備中]][[国司|権守]]、贈[[従四位]] | 幕府 = [[鎌倉幕府]] [[壱岐国|壱岐]][[守護代]] | 主君 = [[惟康親王]] | 氏族 = [[少弐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[少弐経資]] | 兄弟 = '''資時'''、[[少弐盛経|盛経]]<ref name="sonpibunmyaku">『尊卑分脈』第2篇 391頁</ref>、[[少弐時経|時経]]、[[少弐盛氏|盛氏]]、[[少弐貞資|貞資]] | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''少弐 資時'''（しょうに すけとき）は、[[鎌倉時代]]中期の[[武将]]。[[少弐経資]]の長男。[[壱岐国]][[守護代]]。
== 生涯 == [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）の[[元寇|文永の役]]で、叔父・[[少弐景資]]に従って12歳で[[初陣]]を果たした。弘安4年（1281年）の[[元寇|弘安の役]]では、祖父・[[少弐資能|資能]]や父・経資らと共に日本軍を率いて、壱岐島を占領する東路軍との奮闘の末、戦死する。享年19。
この[[元寇|壱岐島の戦い]]で敗退した東路軍は壱岐を放棄して、平戸島に移動した。後に叔父・景資は、[[鷹島 (長崎県)|鷹島]]で蒙古軍を殲滅した。[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）[[11月10日]]、従四位の位階が追贈された<ref>田尻佐 編『贈位諸賢伝 増補版 上』（近藤出版社、1975年）特旨贈位年表 p.34</ref>。
[[壱岐神社]]・壱岐[[護國神社]]（壱岐市[[芦辺町]]）の祭神として祀られており、また、隣接する少弐公園には、「ショウニイさま」と呼ばれていた少弐資時の石積みの墓がある。
==関連史跡== *'''少弐資時の墓'''（壱岐市芦辺町瀬戸浦） :壱岐神社そばに少弐資時の墓がある。周辺は「少弐公園」として整備されており、弘安の役古戦場を示す碑や、当時の日本船のものと思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる[[防人]]の[[狼煙]]台がある。
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|少弐資時の墓 File:Shoni_Park.jpg|小弐公園入口 File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|弘安の役古戦場 File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|碇石 File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|狼煙台 </gallery> *'''船匿城'''(ふなかくしじょう) {{節stub}} ==その他== *[[1997年]]（平成9年）、彫刻家の[[小金丸幾久]]が制作した馬に乗る「少弐資時公像」が、元寇720年を記念して[[芦辺漁港|芦辺港]]フェリーターミナル前に建立された。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} <references/>
== 出典 == *「壱岐の風土と歴史」[[中上史行]]（自費出版）1995年初版、2002年第2版<!-- 壱岐市文化会館蔵 --> *『[[尊卑分脈]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[鷹島_(長崎県)]] *[[少弐景資]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html 少弐資時の墓の旅行] *[http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ 壱岐神社・壱岐護国神社]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しように すけとき}} [[Category:少弐氏|すけとき]] [[Category:鎌倉幕府御家人]] [[Category:守護代]] [[Category:元寇の人物]] [[Category:壱岐国の人物]] [[Category:鎌倉時代に戦死した人物]] [[Category:日本の神 (人物神)]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:1263年生]] [[Category:1281年没]]

Tsunesuke|qid=Q11464740}}|family={{ill|Shōni clan|qid=Q2975368}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11464745]] Wikidata name: Shōni Suketoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User invalid}} [[June 29 (lunar calendar)|June 29]] (July 16, 1281) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | grave = {{ill|Ashibe Town|ja|芦辺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Town|qq=|1=Ashibe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614924}}, Iki City, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] | Rank = {{ill|Governor|ja|権守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}} of [[:en:Bitchū Province|Bitchū Province]], posthumously awarded {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]], Deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] | master = [[:en:Prince Koreyasu|Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}} | siblings = Suketoki, {{ill|Shōni Moritsune|ja|少弐盛経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Moritsune|qq=|1=Shōni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}, {{ill|Shōni Tokitsune|ja|少弐時経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Tokitsune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shōni Morishige|ja|少弐盛氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Morishige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shōni Sadatsune|ja|少弐貞資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sadatsune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = | notable_battles = [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] ({{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|qq=|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|qq=|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == {{Reflist}}

invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|qq=|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>

== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|qq=|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|qq=|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|qq=|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]] | master = [[:en:Prince Koreyasu|Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:Shōni clan|Shōni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. '''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島_(長崎県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860535}} * {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.

== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
