'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-[[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] of [[:en:Iki Province|Iki Province]].
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]] in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to [[:en:Hirado Island|Hirado Island]], and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In [[:en:1915|Taisho 3]] (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the [[:en:Battle of Kōan|Battle of Kōan]], anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a [[:en:Smoke signal|smoke signal]] station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In [[:en:1997|Heisei 9]], a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * "The Climate and History of Iki," {{ill|Nakagami Fumiyuki|ja|中上史行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagami Fumiyuki|1=Shikō Nakagami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362818}} (self-published, 1995, 2002) * [[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島_(長崎県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10860535}} * {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://odekake.jalan.net/spt_42423aj2200024233.html Visit the Grave of Shōni Suketoki] * [http://www.ikiweb.com/ikijinjya/ Iki Shrine and Iki Gokoku Shrine]

== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=smoke signal}} station.

* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=Heisei 9}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}} of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|en|Hirado Island|WD=Q1019313|lt=Hirado Island}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=Taisho 3}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=smoke signal}} station.

* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=Heisei 9}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.

Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}} | era = Mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} | birth_date = {{ill|Kōchō 3|ja|弘長3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchō {{ill|Governor|ja|権守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=kokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}} of {{ill|Bitchū Province|en|Bitchū Province|WD=Q1047514|lt=Bitchū Province}}, posthumously awarded {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}, Deputy {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}} of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}} | master = {{ill|Prince Koreyasu|en|Prince Koreyasu|WD=Q586532|lt=Prince Koreyasu}} | clan = {{ill|Shōni clan|en|Shōni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shōni Sadatsune|ja|少弐貞資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sadatsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki Iki|ja|壱岐島の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}} of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|en|Hirado Island|WD=Q1019313|lt=Hirado Island}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Takashima (Nagasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=Taisho 3}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=smoke signal}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=Heisei 9}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.

'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo|1=Shugo}} of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan|1=Battle of Kōan}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|en|Hirado Island|WD=Q1019313|lt=Hirado Island|1=Hirado Island}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Q11676349|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=Taisho 3|1=1915}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan|1=Battle of Kōan}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=smoke signal|1=Smoke signal}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=Heisei 9|1=1997}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.

parents = Father: {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}} | siblings = Suketoki, {{ill|Shōni Moritsune|ja|少弐盛経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Shōni Suketoki''' (少弐 資時) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} and the eldest son of {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐経資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}, serving as the deputy {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo|1=Shugo}} of {{ill|Iki Province|en|Iki Province|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Province|1=Iki Province}}.
== Biography == In {{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1274), during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Suketoki made his debut in combat at the age of 12, fighting alongside his uncle, {{ill|Shōni Kagesuke|ja|少弐景資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Kagesuke|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan|1=Battle of Kōan}} in {{ill|Kōan 4|ja|弘安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1281), he fought alongside his grandfather {{ill|Shōni Sukenaga|ja|少弐資能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Sukenaga|qq=}}, his father, and other family members, leading Japanese forces against the {{ill|Eastern Route Army|ja|東路軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Route Army|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occupying Iki Island. He died in battle at the age of 19.
Following the defeat of the Eastern Route Army on Iki Island, the Mongol forces retreated to {{ill|Hirado Island|en|Hirado Island|WD=Q1019313|lt=Hirado Island|1=Hirado Island}}, and later, Shōni Kagesuke annihilated the Mongol forces at {{ill|Takashima (Nagasaki)|ja|鷹島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima (Nagasaki)|1=Q11676349|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676349}}. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=Taisho 3|1=1915}} (1915), Suketoki was posthumously awarded the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank.
Suketoki is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ashibe Town, Iki City. A stone grave dedicated to him is located in the adjacent Shōni Park, where he is affectionately known as "Shōni-sama."
== Related Historical Sites == * '''Grave of Shōni Suketoki''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) Located near {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Gokoku Shrine|lt=Iki Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Shrine|qq=}}, the grave is part of "Shōni Park," which also includes a monument commemorating the {{ill|Battle of Kōan|en|Battle of Kōan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of Kōan|1=Battle of Kōan}}, anchor stones believed to be from Japanese ships of the period, and a {{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=smoke signal|1=Smoke signal}} station.
<gallery> File:Shoni_Suketoki's_Tomb_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Grave of Shōni Suketoki File:Shoni_Park.jpg|Entrance to Shōni Park File:Koan_no_Eki_Battleplace_in_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Battlefield of the Kōan Campaign File:Anchor_of_the_Middle-age_Japanese_Ship_in_Shoni_Park,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Anchor stone from the period File:Smoke_Signal_Stand_in_Shoni_Park,_Ashibe,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|Smoke signal station </gallery>
* '''Funakakushi Castle''' {{Section-stub}}
== Additional Information == In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=Heisei 9|1=1997}}, a statue of Shōni Suketoki riding a horse, sculpted by {{ill|Koganemaru Ikku|ja|小金丸幾久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganemaru Ikku|1=Ikuhisa Koganemaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464310}}, was erected in front of the Ashibe Ferry Terminal to commemorate the 720th anniversary of the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.
